items.title: Iran threatens Gulf states‘ energy facilities | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Iran is openly threatening energy facilities in neighboring countries. Tehran has told civilians to evacuate three major ports in the United Arab Emirates, where it claims US forces are sheltering. According to Tehran, they're 'legitimate targets' because of the US/Israeli campaign against Iran. DW talks to Rosemary Kelanic, the Director of the Middle East Program at Defense Priorities.
#dwnews 
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Chicago River turns green for St. Patrick's day | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Chicago River turned bright green as thousands gathered to celebrate St. Patrick’s Day, watching local plumbers pour environmentally friendly dye into the iconic waterway. Across the US, parades brought communities together to honor Irish heritage.

items.title: Protests errupt in Cuba │DW Shorts
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In a rare expression of public anger, protesters vandalized a Communist Party building in central Cuba. As Havana relies heavily on imported fuel for electricity generation, the US oil blockade has brought Cuba's beleaguered economy close to collapse.

#cuba #oilprices #oil #us #dwnews #dwshorts

items.title: Could the US deploy troops to Iran? │DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>As the war enters its third week and the U.S. still has no clear endgame, the possibility of a ground war is raising new fears across the region. DW spoke to a Middle East expert who says the option is still on the table.

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Russia launched deadly overnight strikes on Ukraine, killing at least four people.  There have been almost-daily attacks on Ukraine as the US-brokered peace talks have stalled amid the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Kenyans seek answers on relatives lured into Russia's war | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Families in Kenya are mourning loved ones made to fight in Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine. They want answers from the government about the fate of their sons, fathers, brothers and husbands. Last month, a report released the names of hundreds of Africans who joined the Russian military. More than one in five of them are now believed to be dead. Many others are wounded or missing.

#dwnews #russiaukrainewar #kenya

items.title: Dust storm sweeps Gaza │DW Shorts
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A dust storm swept across Gaza, turning the skies into orange-tinted color. While dust storms occur in Gaza, there hasn’t been one as intense as this for more than five years. Residents were warned to take shelter and secure their tents from being blown away. Other parts of the Middle East were also affected.

#dwnews #dwshorts #gaza #duststorm #sand #middleeast #environment

items.title: US strikes Kharg Island, key Iran oil export hub
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>LIVE NOW: Week 3 of the War on Iran

Is the US preparing to put boots on the ground inside Iran? In the last 24 hours, US forces have launched fresh airstrikes on Kharg Island — Iran's crucial oil hub. Donald Trump says the island’s military sites were "obliterated," while its oil facilities remain untouched — for now.

#dwnews  #iranwar #oil 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Iran war hits India's kitchens | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>As the US-Israel-Iran conflict escalates, India is beginning to feel the economic shockwaves thousands of miles away. Cooking gas supplies are tightening in New Delhi, with LPG shortages and rising prices affecting homes and restaurants. The government has invoked emergency measures to boost production as fears grow that supplies could run dangerously low.

#dwnews #iranwar #india
